summary.intent: Implement task #295: a framework-agnostic SignaturePad core component (state machine, stroke data model, export) for the ars-ui Rust library, then iteratively expand to include an injected raster export capability with a web browser implementation.
summary.outcome: Fully implemented and committed the SignaturePad agnostic core (Idle/Drawing/Completed state machine, 64+ unit/snapshot/proptest tests, 99.7% coverage), a SignatureRasterizer capability trait in ars-core with a pressure-aware WebSignatureRasterizer in ars-dom supporting PNG/JPEG/WebP with honest format detection, plus complete spec sync across core and adapter specs. All xci-fast gates passed before commit.

repo: The ars-core engine uses typed Effect enum markers (PendingEffect::named(Effect::X)) interpreted by adapters — the spec for signature-pad had drifted to use string-named effects and the adapter-only use_platform_effects() hook, making the spec non-compiling against the real engine.

All five sibling component specs (clipboard, timer, color-picker, color-area, angle-slider) include on_props_changed prop-sync; any new component must include it or the controlled prop is non-functional after init.

ars-components and ars-dom are sibling crates — neither depends on the other; both depend on ars-core. Capability traits (like SignatureRasterizer, PlatformEffects) must be defined in ars-core with neutral types so ars-dom can implement them without depending on ars-components.

spec-compile-snippets uses syn::parse_file (syntax-only), so semantic bugs in spec code blocks (missing type aliases, wrong method names) do not fail the gate — lint-code is a legacy tool, not an xci gate.

canvas.toDataURL() silently falls back to image/png for unsupported formats (Safari + WebP) per the HTML spec; RasterImage.format must be derived from the returned data URL's actual MIME, not assumed from the requested format.

ColorValue in ars-core is HSL+alpha with a hex-only parser — it cannot represent named colors, var(), currentColor, or modern CSS color syntax, making it unsuitable as the type for CSS pen color pass-through.
The project runs wasm browser tests via wasm-pack test --headless --chrome; ars-dom wasm coverage is measured by a separate nightly wasm pipeline, not by xci-fast.
Snapshot orphan detection must include --features i18n, otherwise highlight's case-fold snapshots appear unreferenced (they are feature-gated).

code.finding: The let-chain false branch in DrawMove (defensive guard ensuring current_stroke is Some) is genuinely unreachable in the Drawing state — current_stroke is always populated — but is kept as a defensive guard. Only uncovered line in the module.

llvm-cov attributes Clone/Debug derive impls as missed functions even after explicit clone/debug/eq test calls due to LLVM derive-attribution quirk. These show as ~6 missed functions but represent no real logic gap; aggregate coverage gate is unaffected.

web-sys 0.3.99 canvas API: stroke/fill style setters are set_stroke_style_str/set_fill_style_str (renamed from older versions). Always verify exact web-sys method names against docs.rs for the resolved version before writing wasm code.

workflow: When a branch has no local commits, rebase onto updated main by stashing uncommitted WIP (git stash --include-untracked), fast-forwarding, then popping the stash — avoids rebase conflicts on unstaged changes.

For cross-crate capability injection (trait in ars-core, impl in ars-dom), build the agnostic ars-core types first with tests, then add ars-components integration, then implement the ars-dom web backend — TDD layering that isolates compilation failures by crate.

Fetch resolved dependency version (cargo metadata or Cargo.lock) and verify exact API from docs.rs before writing any web-sys bindings — external APIs change across minor versions and wrong names produce hard-to-debug wasm link errors.

xci-fast is the mandatory pre-push gate (~10-15 min); it includes fmt, clippy -D warnings, unit/integration/adapter tests, spec-compile-snippets, error-variant-coverage, and native coverage gate (threshold 75/70 line/function).

Derive-generated Clone/Debug impls are not exercised by assert_eq! (which only calls Debug on failure) — add explicit clone/format!/ne assertions to close coverage on derived types when the crate has a coverage gate.

friction: Spec §1.5 was non-compiling drift against the real engine (wrong Effect API, adapter-only hook in agnostic code) — required significant pre-implementation investigation to identify the gap before planning.

Post-audit found two silent contract violations in both the spec and the initial implementation (H1: Undo leaving Completed+empty strokes; H2: DrawEnd on discarded tap entering Completed with spurious announcement) — these required additional transition logic, test updates, proptest invariant changes, and spec edits.
Missing on_props_changed was a spec omission discovered only by comparing all five sibling specs — the controlled data prop was silently non-functional after init.
clippy -D warnings promoted an unused import in ars-core raster tests to an error, requiring a fix after the native clippy pass appeared clean.
Stale doc comments describing an earlier 'adapter superset format enum' design were found just before commit and required cleanup — doc drift from iterative design changes during the session.
Fully-qualified ars_core:: paths were flagged by clippy as unnecessary after rustfmt reformatted the test additions, requiring a secondary fix pass.
llvm-cov's derive-attribution caused coverage to appear to drop to 76% on a per-crate filtered run, requiring investigation to confirm it was measurement noise rather than a real gap.
open_items: PR not yet opened on GitHub at transcript end — was the next step after commit.
ars-dom wasm coverage not measured by xci-fast — covered by the separate nightly wasm CI pipeline.

Adapter implementations for SignaturePad (Leptos, Dioxus) are future tasks; adapter specs now document the SignatureRasterizer injection point and WebSignatureRasterizer as the browser implementation.
CssColor typed wrapper for exhaustive CSS color representation was discussed and deferred as a separate foundation-level design task — decision: keep pen_color as String for now.

The injected SignatureRasterizer pattern for non-browser (native/SSR) rasterization via tiny-skia was noted as a future possibility but not implemented — NullSignatureRasterizer returns Unsupported for those environments.
